description: Technical lesson log for deeper side-paths explored during the Pokemon Card Display project.

lessons.problem: How to render pokemon cards dynamically without relying only on innerHTML string templating.
lessons.pattern: createElement -> configure node -> render inner content -> appendChild
notes: createElement creates a DOM node in memory.
The node is not yet visible in the page after creation.
appendChild inserts the node into the DOM tree, making it visible inside its parent container.
dataset can attach useful metadata like data-name without hardcoding it directly into the HTML string.
For this small project, innerHTML would also have been a valid practical solution. This lesson was explored to understand the DOM more deeply, not because the simpler approach was wrong.

lessons.topic: Decorative UI via data attributes and pseudo-elements
lessons.problem: How to display a pokemon primary type badge on cards without adding extra HTML elements.
lessons.pattern: dataset in JS -> CSS attribute selector -> ::after pseudo-element
notes: JavaScript attached semantic metadata to the card via data-type.
CSS used attribute selectors to react to that metadata.
The ::after pseudo-element created a decorative badge without modifying the HTML structure.
This pattern works well for decorative UI elements such as badges, icons, or status indicators.
It becomes less appropriate when the information represents core content rather than decoration.
In this project, the pattern was later removed because pokemon types are real content in the dialog view and therefore better rendered as HTML.
